attributes.action: Investigate academy trust finances and staffing decisions
attributes.additional_details: Frontline school staff are being reduced despite already stretched resources, impacting children’s support and wellbeing. At the same time, there is limited transparency around trust spending. Parents deserve accountability for decisions that directly affect their children.
attributes.background: Introduce independent oversight of academy trusts, require transparency on finances and staffing, and investigate the impact of frontline staff cuts. Ensure funding is prioritised for children and that trusts are held accountable for decisions affecting pupils.

rejection.details:
Petitions need to call on the Government or Parliament to take a specific action.

We're not sure exactly what you'd like the Government or Parliament to do.

There are already mandatory rules on financial management and transparency for Academy Trusts. You can find out more here: 
https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/academy-trust-handbook/academy-trust-handbook-2025-effective-from-1-september-2025

We could accept a petition calling for new rules, as long as it was clear how these would be different to the ones that already exists. You may wish to sign this petition: 

Launch a public inquiry into how public funds are spent in education: https://petition.parliament.uk/petitions/762390
